name: Additionality
rationale: A credit that represents an emission reduction that would have happened anyway has zero climate value. Additionality is the most contested yet most important integrity dimension.
data_source: Project design document + financial analysis + common practice review (oracle required)

bands.label: Very High
bands.criteria: Carbon revenue is >50% of project income; technology is pre-commercial; no regulatory mandate; financial analysis shows negative NPV without carbon finance

bands.label: High
bands.criteria: Carbon revenue is 30-50% of income; clear investment barrier documented; regulatory surplus demonstrated

bands.label: Moderate
bands.criteria: Carbon revenue is 15-30% of income; multiple revenue streams; common practice analysis supports not-yet-standard

bands.label: Marginal
bands.criteria: Carbon revenue <15% of income; project type becoming standard practice; relies primarily on barrier analysis

bands.label: Low
bands.criteria: Project type is common in jurisdiction; weak barrier analysis; regulatory requirements approaching project standard

bands.label: Questionable
bands.criteria: Project type is mandated or standard practice; no credible barrier analysis; free-rider concerns

red_flags.condition: Project was already under construction before carbon finance was secured
Government subsidies cover >80% of project costs
Technology is the cheapest available option regardless of carbon revenue

notes: Scoring requires off-chain expert evaluation; attested via oracle.
Calyx Global's additive scoring model is recommended: positive in one area cannot overcome risk in another.
